title: Self-hosting FSL toolchain
manager: The dogfood crown: rewrite FSL's own lexer, parser, checker, and compiler as FSL machines that FSL can then verify. Today the toolchain is hand-written TypeScript the language cannot reason about; self-hosting turns the compiler pipeline into state machines whose correctness properties are checkable by the very prover the language ships. It is both the ultimate demo — the language proves its own plumbing — and a forcing function that exposes every place FSL is still too weak to describe real control flow.
cs: lexer/parser/checker/compiler expressed as verifiable FSL; correctness properties fed to the same checker used for user machines; success gated on the emitted toolchain passing its own proofs.
